channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: Iran Deploys Internet Blackout During Military Conflict; Starlink Terminals Targeted
item.description: Iran shut down internet access during Operation Epic Fury military strikes, while regime reportedly targets Iranians using smuggled Starlink terminals for circumvention.

item.title: Congress Plans Clean FISA 702 Extension Despite Mass Surveillance Abuses
item.description: Congress is moving toward an 18-month clean extension of FISA Section 702 mass surveillance powers without warrant requirements or other reforms, despite documented abuses against activists, journalists, and lawmakers.

item.title: Flock ALPR Cameras Used for Traffic Enforcement Despite Company Claims Otherwise
item.description: Georgia police used Flock Safety's license plate readers to ticket a motorcyclist for holding a phone, contradicting the company's public claims that ALPRs aren't used for traffic violations.

item.title: Tails 7.6 Adds Automatic Tor Bridge Discovery for Censorship Circumvention
item.description: Tails 7.6 introduces automatic Tor bridge configuration based on user region, making censorship circumvention easier for activists and journalists in restrictive networks.

item.title: Hungarian Journalist Faces Espionage Investigation After Pegasus Spyware Targeting
item.description: Hungary orders espionage investigation of journalist Szabolcs Panyi following covert surveillance and Pegasus spyware targeting, escalating government crackdown on independent media.

item.title: EFF's Cindy Cohn Releases Book on 30-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance
item.description: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn published 'Privacy's Defender,' documenting three decades of legal battles against government surveillance including the Crypto Wars and NSA dragnet programs.

item.title: Supreme Court Limits ISP Copyright Liability, Protects General-Purpose Internet Services
item.description: Supreme Court reversed billion-dollar verdict against Cox Communications, ruling ISPs can't be liable for customer copyright infringement unless they actively induce it or provide services tailored for infringement.

item.title: Apple Introduces OS-Level Age Verification in UK, Creating Identity-Based Access Infrastructure
item.description: Apple now requires credit card or government ID verification for UK iPhone users to access certain features, moving age checks from apps into the operating system itself and creating infrastructure for broader identity-based restrictions.

item.title: Anti-ICE Protesters Convicted on Terrorism Charges for Encrypted Comms and Black Clothing
item.description: Eight protesters convicted of terrorism charges for wearing black clothing and using Signal messaging during ICE facility demonstration, marking first federal material support charges against alleged antifa members.

item.title: Italian Prosecutors Confirm Journalist Hacked with Paragon 'Ethical' Spyware
item.description: Italian prosecutors validated Citizen Lab's forensics showing journalist Francesco Cancellato was targeted by Paragon's Graphite spyware, undermining claims of 'ethical' commercial surveillance tools.

item.title: Survey: 78% of US Parents Concerned About Children's Digital Privacy
item.description: Proton survey reveals widespread parental anxiety about children's online privacy, with 58% fearing identity theft before age 18 and concerns about data collection by apps and schools.

item.title: UK Advances Social Media Ban with Ministerial Powers to Restrict Internet Access for Under-18s
item.description: UK Parliament is progressing legislation that would give ministers sweeping powers to ban social media for under-18s and restrict VPN use without accountability mechanisms or evidence requirements.

item.title: Pakistan Uses Colonial-Era Law to Suppress Women's Rights March, Detains 60+ Activists
item.description: Pakistani authorities detained over 60 activists and journalists attempting to hold the annual Aurat March on International Women's Day, using Section 144 restrictions and confiscating phones.

item.title: The Intercept Guide: Protecting Devices from ICE/CBP Searches at US Airports
item.description: Comprehensive security guide for protecting digital devices from Immigration and Customs Enforcement and border agents at US airports, covering throwaway devices, encryption, and operational security practices.

item.title: EFF Analyzes Digital Legacy of Arab Spring: From Liberation Tool to Repression Infrastructure
item.description: EFF launches series examining how 2011 Arab uprisings transformed digital resistance tactics and government countermeasures, showing how liberation tools became surveillance infrastructure.

item.title: EFF Sues to Free Safety Laws from Copyright Restrictions
item.description: EFF filed lawsuit against CPSC demanding public access to legally binding safety codes that private organizations claim copyright over even after incorporation into federal law.

item.title: EFF Challenges FCC Chair's Unconstitutional Threats Against Broadcasters
item.description: EFF and civil liberties groups condemn FCC Chair Brendan Carr's threats to revoke broadcast licenses for unfavorable coverage of Trump, calling them unconstitutional censorship attempts.

item.title: Citizen Lab Submits Digital Transnational Repression Framework to UN
item.description: Citizen Lab submitted recommendations to the UN on digital transnational repression - governments using digital tools to surveil and silence human rights defenders across borders.

item.title: Japan's Team Mirai Uses Open-Source AI Tools to Reshape Democratic Participation
item.description: Japan's Team Mirai party won 11 seats using open-source AI tools for direct voter engagement, policy formation, and transparency - demonstrating scalable digital democracy infrastructure.

item.title: NYC Ends $4M Palantir Hospital Contract After Activist Pressure
item.description: New York City's public hospital system will not renew its $4 million Palantir contract in October after sustained activist pressure against the surveillance company's presence in healthcare.

item.title: EFF's 2026 Foilies Expose Government Transparency Failures
item.description: EFF and MuckRock's annual "Foilies" highlight worst government transparency violations, including Texas hiding Abbott-Musk communications and DHS abandoning text message archiving.

item.title: Former Coinbase CTO Calls for Crypto Tools for Refugees and Stateless People
item.description: Balaji Srinivasan advocates for crypto industry to build financial tools serving refugees and stateless populations amid rising global conflicts and migration.

item.title: Major Publishers Block Internet Archive Over AI Concerns, Threatening Historical Web Record
item.description: The New York Times and other publishers are blocking Internet Archive crawlers due to AI scraping fears, potentially erasing decades of preserved web history that researchers and journalists depend on.

item.title: EFF Urges Supreme Court to Hold Cisco Liable for Torture-Enabling Surveillance System
item.description: EFF filed Supreme Court brief arguing Cisco should face liability for custom-building China's 'Golden Shield' surveillance system used to persecute, torture Falun Gong practitioners.

item.title: EFF Backs Minnesota Lawmaker Against Internet Age Verification Mandate
item.description: EFF supports Rep. Leigh Finke's opposition to Minnesota's HF1434 age verification bill, which would require government ID/biometric verification for broad categories of online speech beyond explicit content.

item.title: Sen. Wyden Warns of Classified Section 702 Surveillance Abuse
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden revealed existence of classified Section 702 surveillance abuse that affects Americans' privacy rights, warning Congress is debating reauthorization without full information.

item.title: Iran Targets Amazon Data Centers in First Military Strike on Commercial Cloud Infrastructure
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard struck Amazon data centers in UAE and Bahrain, marking first military attack on commercial cloud infrastructure due to its dual military-civilian use supporting Pentagon operations.

item.title: EFF Sues Medicare for Records on AI System Evaluating Patient Care Requests
item.description: EFF filed FOIA lawsuit against CMS seeking transparency on WISeR program that uses AI to evaluate Medicare prior authorizations, affecting 6.4M seniors with potential bias and denial risks.

item.title: EFF Warns of Privacy Risks in Meta's Smart Glasses Surge
item.description: EFF highlights serious privacy concerns as Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses go mainstream, including human review of footage and covert recording capabilities that threaten bystander privacy.

item.title: Court Blocks Pentagon Retaliation Against AI Company Refusing Surveillance Role
item.description: Federal court granted preliminary injunction protecting Anthropic from Pentagon retaliation after company refused to remove AI guardrails for government surveillance operations.

item.title: EFF Warns of Privacy Risks in Mainstream Smartglasses Adoption
item.description: EFF highlights privacy concerns with Meta Ray-Bans and other mainstream smartglasses, warning that third parties may access recorded content beyond just device owners.

item.title: Nicole Ozer Named New EFF Executive Director, Bringing Anti-Surveillance Expertise
item.description: Nicole Ozer, architect of California's strongest electronic surveillance laws, will lead EFF starting June 1, succeeding Cindy Cohn after 25+ years with the organization.

item.title: Judge Blocks DOD Blacklisting of Anthropic as First Amendment Retaliation
item.description: Federal judge granted preliminary injunction blocking Department of War's blacklisting of Anthropic, ruling it was unconstitutional retaliation for the company's critical press statements.
